parentGate.title: For Parents
parentGate.description: To continue, please solve this math problem:

hero.title: Built for our daughter. Shared with your family.
hero.subtitle: Lepdy started as one parent’s way to teach their child Hebrew at home. Today it’s a free, ad-free place where children aged 2–7 fall in love with the Alef-Bet — at their own pace, in a real voice.

founder.title: Made by a parent. Voiced by a real child.
founder.body: Every letter, number and word you hear is spoken by Noa, a real Israeli girl — not a synthetic voice. We wanted children to hear Hebrew the way it’s actually spoken at home: warm and natural, from the very first tap.

preview.title: A peek inside
preview.subtitle: Big, colorful cards. One tap plays the sound. That’s the whole idea.

learn.title: What your child will explore
learn.subtitle: Six worlds to discover, each one a tap away.

games.title: Learning that feels like play
games.body: Ten little games turn practice into something kids ask to do again — matching, memory, counting, catching falling letters, even tracing their first Hebrew shapes.

authentic.title: A real voice
authentic.description: Native pronunciation by an Israeli child, not a robot.

free.title: Free and ad-free
free.description: No ads, no pop-ups, nothing to buy. A calm place to learn.

noLogin.title: No sign-up needed
noLogin.description: Open the page and start. No account, no email, no download.

everywhere.title: Works on any screen
everywhere.description: Phone, tablet or computer — the cards resize to fit.

independent.title: Kids can explore alone
independent.description: Clear pictures and audio mean little ones don’t need to read to play.

rewards.title: Rewards that motivate
rewards.description: Stickers and a growing word collection keep children coming back.

how.title: How children learn with Lepdy
how.intro: Young children learn best through their senses and through play. Lepdy is built around one simple loop, repeated often:

listen.title: Listen
listen.description: Tap a card to hear the word in a clear, real voice.

see.title: See
see.description: A bold image and color anchor the word in memory.

play.title: Play
play.description: Games turn fresh words into something kids practice for fun.

collect.title: Collect
collect.description: Stickers and saved words celebrate every step forward.

how.note: No pressure, no scores to beat — just curiosity, repeated often.

faq1.question: What age is Lepdy for?
faq1.answer: Lepdy is designed for children aged 2 to 7. Toddlers enjoy tapping cards and hearing sounds, while older kids dive into the games and start recognizing letters and numbers on their own.

faq2.question: How long should a session be?
faq2.answer: Short and often works best at this age — about 10 to 15 minutes. Frequent little visits beat one long sitting.

faq3.question: Do I need to know Hebrew to help?
faq3.answer: Not at all. Every letter, number and word plays its own pronunciation, so you and your child can learn together from scratch.

faq4.question: Is Lepdy really free?
faq4.answer: Yes — completely free, with no ads and no account required. Just open the site and start playing.

faq5.question: Can my child use it alone?
faq5.answer: Yes. Clear pictures, audio cues and simple navigation let children explore at their own pace, with or without a grown-up nearby.

faq6.question: What devices does it work on?
faq6.answer: Any modern device with a browser — phone, tablet or computer. There’s nothing to download or install.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.subtitle: Open the Alef-Bet and let your child take the first tap.

safety.intro: At Lepdy, we take child safety and privacy very seriously. Our app is designed to provide a safe, educational environment for young learners.
safety.noAdsText: Lepdy is completely ad-free. Children can learn without interruptions or exposure to inappropriate content.
safety.noDataCollectionText: We do not ask children to submit personal information during normal play. Some parent-facing features and site analytics may process limited data as described in our Privacy Policy.
safety.parentalControlsText: Parent-only sections are protected by a simple math gate that children typically cannot solve, ensuring parents maintain control over app settings and information pages.
safety.offlineCapableText: The app contains only age-appropriate educational content carefully designed for children and families.
safety.coppaCompliantText: Parents can review our Privacy Policy and contact us with questions about data handling, cloud sync, or deletion requests.

contact.intro: We'd love to hear from you! Whether you have questions, suggestions, or feedback about Lepdy, please don't hesitate to reach out.
contact.feedbackText: Your feedback helps us improve Lepdy for all families. Let us know what features you'd like to see, report any issues, or share your child's learning success stories.

learnPage.heading: How to Teach Kids Hebrew at Home
learnPage.intro: Teaching Hebrew to children is far easier when it happens through play. Lepdy offers a simple, fun way to teach your child letters, numbers, colors, shapes and animals — all with authentic pronunciation by a real Israeli child. Here is a short guide to help you get started.
learnPage.step1Text: Introduce your child to the 22 Alef Bet letters, one at a time, with clear pronunciation.
learnPage.step2Text: Learn to count from 1 to 10 in Hebrew using pictures and sounds.
learnPage.step3Text: Expand vocabulary with everyday colors and shapes children already recognize.
learnPage.step4Text: Reinforce knowledge through fun educational games made for young children.
learnPage.topicsTitle: What you can learn on Lepdy
learnPage.tip1: Learn a little every day — a few consistent minutes beat one long lesson.
learnPage.tip2: Review yesterday's words before moving forward.
learnPage.tip3: Let your child tap and listen on their own — active learning sticks.
